mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.4 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the cross-sectional IC drops below zero consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit extension reflects broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both proxies rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with accelerating consumer price inflation compresses real profit margins through input cost pass-through lags, depressing equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when industrial output and CPI both accelerate, or the joint signal fails to predict the top quartile of forward returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An aggressive federal funds rate hike cycle increases borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5 percentage points in a single quarter
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive average returns in the 20 days following rate hikes exceeding the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the corporate earnings base, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over a one-week window.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth accelerates, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC never exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction that erodes consumer purchasing power and corporate sales, depressing equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when payroll growth and unemployment rise together, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening aggregate supply and potential demand destruction, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered contraction episodes, or the signal fails to rank in the top 20% of predictive features in a multivariate model.
